title: Canvas ink is low-contrast (grey on grey), light + dark mode
description:
The user's handwriting/ink on the trace canvas is very low contrast — grey strokes on the grey/paper canvas, hard to read. The user reports it happens in BOTH light AND dark mode; the fix must be verified in both appearances. (User, 2026-07-19, iPhone session screenshot — the drawn こ strokes are barely visible against the paper.)

Anchors: the pen is set in Sources/Features/Session/Canvas/WritingCanvas.swift:33 — canvasView.tool = PKInkingTool(.pen, color: KakitoriTheme.inkUIColor, width: Self.penWidth) (penWidth = 6). The canvas is a PKCanvasView with drawingPolicy = .anyInput, backgroundColor = .clear, isOpaque = false (WritingCanvas.swift ~29-38); updateUIView is EMPTY (WritingCanvas.swift:45), so the tool/color is never refreshed after creation (e.g. on a light/dark trait change). KakitoriTheme.inkUIColor is a DYNAMIC UIColor { trait -> inkLight #1A1A1A (near-black) in light, inkDark #F0EDE6 (near-white) in dark } (Sources/Support/Theme.swift:9-20). So the ink SHOULD be near-black in light mode and near-white in dark — yet it renders mid-grey and low-contrast in both, which is the bug.

Candidate causes to investigate (NOT resolving here): (1) PencilKit resolves the trait-based dynamic UIColor once at tool-creation against a base/unspecified traitCollection (not the canvas's actual appearance), landing on a mid value; and because updateUIView never re-sets the tool, it also won't track appearance changes. (2) PencilKit's built-in light/dark color ADAPTATION/inversion for strokes can lighten a dark ink (it stores stroke colors keyed to an appearance and adapts them to the current one), so a near-black ink drawn over a clear canvas can display as grey. (3) The .pen ink tool has velocity/pressure-driven opacity which can make strokes look faint. A likely fix direction is to pass a RESOLVED (non-dynamic), high-contrast color for the current appearance (resolve inkUIColor against the canvas traitCollection), refresh the tool in updateUIView on trait changes, and/or pin the canvas appearance (overrideUserInterfaceStyle) / use a constant-opacity tool — but which of these is the real fix needs testing.

Scope/notes: layer is canvas (PencilKit, per the project's layer conventions). This is a CONTRAST/color issue, not Apple-Pencil ink FEEL, so a simulator screenshot CAN verify it (it is NOT the needs-human M1 ink-tuning gate). Acceptance must confirm the user's strokes are clearly high-contrast against the paper in BOTH light and dark mode. Applies to iPhone + iPad (same WritingCanvas). The faded trace GUIDES (KakitoriTheme.inkFaint, GuideBox.swift:48) are intentionally low-contrast — don't confuse them with the user's own ink, which is what must be darkened/brightened.

title: Retired Tofugu decks linger after Foundations swap (5 decks, want 3)
description:
Home shows FIVE deck cards but should show only THREE: the two old Tofugu decks (Tofugu Hiragana Anki Deck ~101 cards, Tofugu Katakana Anki Deck ~124 cards) are still present alongside the three new Foundations decks (Hiragana 104, Katakana 104, Kanji 87). The user wants the Tofugu ones gone. (User, 2026-07-19, iPhone Home; banner reads "510 characters to write across 3 scripts", inflated by the leftover decks.)

Root cause: this is a data-migration gap introduced by the Foundations deck swap. BundledDeckLoader (Sources/App/BundledDeckLoader.swift) bumped bundleVersion 1 -> 2 and now imports the Foundations source split into three decks (sourceDeckName "Kakitori Foundations::Hiragana" / "::Katakana" / "::Kanji"). But load() only ADDS/updates the currently-bundled decks (matched by sourceDeckName via ApkgImporter.apply); it NEVER removes decks that are no longer part of the bundle. The retired Tofugu decks were imported at version 1 with sourceDeckName "Tofugu Hiragana Anki Deck" / "Tofugu Katakana Anki Deck" (the raw Anki top-deck names, left unchanged), so they persist in the on-disk SwiftData store. The version gate (isUpToDate + UserDefaults key "loadedBundledDecksVersion") only controls whether the import RUNS, not pruning.

Who is affected: any install that had the Tofugu decks (version 1) before the swap now shows 5 decks; a FRESH install (version 0 -> 2) imports only the 3 Foundations decks and shows 3, which is why the earlier verification screenshots (taken after an uninstall) looked correct and this slipped through. The user did not reinstall, so they see the leftovers.

Fix direction (NOT resolving here): on a bundle-version bump, PRUNE decks that are no longer produced by the current bundled set — e.g. delete any Deck whose sourceDeckName is not one the current bundle would create (or specifically remove the known-retired Tofugu decks). Deleting a Deck should cascade to its Sections/Notes/CardSchedules (SwiftData delete rules). No user scheduling needs preserving for the removed Tofugu decks. Consider making the loader own an explicit "retired sourceDeckNames" list, or a general reconcile step that removes decks not in the current bundled-source set. Anchors: BundledDeckLoader.load (~L40-65), deckResourceNames = ["Foundations"], sectionTitles keyed Hiragana/Katakana/Kanji; ApkgImporter.apply matches by sourceDeckName. Related: the Foundations swap commits (feat/foundations-deck), and idea #? / T060 (daily-allowance banner) is separate. Applies to both iPhone and iPad (same loader/store).
